examples.title: Linked Stack Implementation in C
examples.description: Complete implementation of stack using linked list with push, pop, display, and isEmpty operations
examples.explanation: This C program implements a linked stack. The push function allocates a new node, sets its data, links it to current top, and updates top pointer. The pop function checks for underflow, retrieves data, advances top to next node, and frees the old node. Display traverses from top until NULL.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for push and pop, O(n) for display
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is number of elements, plus O(1) auxiliary space

examples.title: Linked Queue Implementation in Python
examples.description: Implementation of queue using linked list with enqueue, dequeue, and display operations
examples.explanation: This Python program implements a linked queue. The enqueue method adds elements at the rear - when queue is empty, both front and rear point to new node; otherwise rear's next is updated. The dequeue method removes from front - if front becomes None after removal, rear is also set to None. Both operations are O(1).
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for enqueue and dequeue, O(n) for display
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for n queue elements, O(1) auxiliary

examples.title: Linked Stack Implementation in Java
examples.description: Object-oriented implementation of stack using linked list in Java
examples.explanation: This Java program implements a generic linked stack using generics. The Node class stores data and next pointer. LinkedStack class provides push, pop, peek, isEmpty, and display methods. Java's garbage collector automatically handles memory deallocation when nodes are no longer referenced.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for push, pop, peek; O(n) for display
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for n stack elements plus O(1) auxiliary
